content: ESA Certified Mold Inspector & Certified Mold Assessor (CMI & CMA) #11291
The following items or discoveries indicate that these systems or components do not function as intended or adversely
affects the habitability of the dwelling; or warrants further investigation by a specialist, or requires subsequent
observation. This summary shall not contain recommendations for routine upkeep of a system or component to keep it in
proper functioning condition or recommendations to upgrade or enhance the function or efficiency of the building. This
Summary is not the entire report. The complete report may include additional information of concern to the customer. It is
recommended that the customer read the complete report.
The goal of an inspection is to identify significant issues than would affect the average persons decision to purchase a
property. During the course of an inspection we often identify minor defects which are also included. When you move into a
property you may find issues that are not identified. This can be expected and we suggest you allow roughly 1% of the value
annually for this type of maintenance and repair.
(1) The roof covering shows heavy granular loss and wear and the underlying shingle matrix was exposed in some
areas. The life expectancy of the covering seems to be deteriorating at an accelerated rate. The covering does not
need to be replaced in the short term but should be planned for in a few years. While this covering should last a few
more years, this system needs minor repair and should be monitored every year for replacement.
(2) There was some damaged shingles in a rear valley. Recommend having a roofing contractor repair or replace.
(3) There was some damage to shingles at the roof cap. Recommend having a roofing contractor repair as needed.
The flashing at the front gable has been nailed and those nail heads are lacking caulk. Recommend having a
contractor seal all nail heads to prevent roof leaks.
(1) The chimney is very old and the fireplace is an old coal burning model. Recommend sealing off and not using
this fireplace and chimney for safety.
(2) There are open areas where trim, siding, and brick chimney meet. Recommend having a contractor seal and
(3) There is a repaired area of shingles around a plumbing vent on the roof. Recommend having a roofing
contractor further evaluate and repair if needed.
(1) Mildew water intrusion was noted around the building at various location the masonry block showed signs of
mildew and potentially water intrusion due to the splash back from the storm water not being properly controlled and
running over the roof edge. Recommend having a gutter installation company install gutters around the building and
(2) The few gutters are full of debris in areas and needs to be cleaned. The debris in gutters can also conceal rust,
deterioration or leaks that are not visible until cleaned, and we are unable to determine if such conditions exist.
The shakes under the front gable vent are water damaged. Recommend having a siding contractor repair or replace
(1) Trim at the rear deck door was noted as deteriorated on both sides. Recommend having a contractor
evaluate and properly repair or replace as needed.
(2) Exterior doors also should be openable from the inside without the use of a key or special knowledge for
As per IPMC 702.3 AND the IFC 2006 1008.1.8 All means of egress doors shall be readily openable from the side
from which egress is to be made without the need for keys, special knowledge or effort.
Replace double keyed deadbolts with thumb latch type for safety.
(1) The window sills and trim around the planters at the front of the home are showing signs of water damage.
(2) The wooden exterior window trim and cement siding around the home were noted as in need of painting. All
exterior paintable surfaces should be coated in a quality weather resistant paint and maintained periodically.
Typically painting is needed every 5 years or so. Recommend having a qualified painting company paint
exterior wooden windows and trim on the home.
(3) The damage on the left side bathroom window was noted as having gone beyond the trim into the jamb
assembly and was in need of extensive repairs and potentially replacement of the whole window assembly.
Recommend having a contractor evaluate and repair or replace as needed.
DECKS, BALCONIES, STOOPS, STEPS, AREAWAYS, PORCHES, PATIO/ COVER AND APPLICABLE
(1) The stairs leading up to the back deck lacked a graspable handrail. All stairways should have a graspable
handrail to provide safe access for all occupants and visitors. Recommend installing a circular, graspable handrail
for the stairs as required for safety.
(2) Currently the back deck steps have open risers spaced more than 4 inches apart. This could be potential hazard
to small children. Recommend having a deck builder install risers on steps so that the spacing is less than 4 inches
(3) The rear deck was noted as weathered and some boards were cracked and minor splitting was noted. The deck
overall was in good shape. Treated wood if left unprotected has a limited life expectancy. Recommend having a
contractor wash and protect deck with a wood preservative for safety.
(2) There were two loose wires on the exterior of the home. Recommend having an electrician remove if no longer
(3) The exterior receptacle on the rear deck was noted as loose and no longer properly secured to the wall.
Recommend having an electrician repair as needed.
(4) There is an extension cord running from the rear outlet to the crawlspace. Extension cords are for temporary use
only. Recommend having an electrician run an outlet to the area where needed.
(5) The lights located on the exterior of home should be sealed to prevent water intrusion. "Fixtures installed in wet
or damp locations shall be installed so that water cannot enter or accumulate in wiring compartments, lampholders
or other electrical parts". Recommend having a handy man seal the luminaries to prevent insect and water intrusion
(1) The exterior hose bib did not have a siphon protection device installed. Recommend installing this inexpensive
device to prevent back siphonage from a connected hose.
VEGETATION, GRADING, DRAINAGE, DRIVEWAYS, PARKING LOTS, WALKWAYS AND RETAINING WALLS
(With respect to their effect on the condition of the building)
(1) Bushes around the building are very close to the structure and may be having a negative impact on the wall
The limited clearance of the shrubbery and the siding can cause moisture buildup and moisture problems within the
Recommend having all bushes and shrubs a minimum of 12 inches away from the structure for adequate air flow
and to prevent damage to siding and trim components.
(2) The driveway and sidewalk were noted as showing signs of cracking and minor settling in various locations.
Recommend having the driveway and sidewalk sealed or repaired as a part of preventative maintenance and
(1) Fascia boards at the roof transitions were in contact with shingles and showed signs of water damage from
wicking. Recommend having a contractor repair or replace, and add flashing as needed to prevent future damage.
(2) Soffits and fascia at the front of the home are noted as having water stains and showed water damage and were
Recommend having a contractor repair or replace all damaged fascia and soffit.
(1) There is a bird's nest on top of a column at the front porch. Recommend having nest removed and placing anti-
perching strips on top of the columns to prevent future nesting.
(2) The fence surrounding the property was noted as in disrepair and in need of being replaced. Recommend
having a fence installer evaluate and repair or replace as needed.
There was some minor damage to drywall ceiling at hallway bath is damaged. Recommend having repaired and
The old coal fireplace was noted as closed off and inoperable at the time of the inspection. It was a simple
decorative feature to the home at the time of the inspection.
There were several paint cans found in the laundry room closet. Paint is flammable and should be removed from
(1) A radon test was set up in the home at 2:49 PM on the day of the inspection and will be picked up in 48 hours or
(1) There were signs of previous or current water intrusion on the exterior and interior of the right side foundation
walls at the driveway and in the crawlspace. There were signs of organic growth on the exterior wall and at the
crawlspace vents and some efflorescence on the interior of the crawlspace wall. Recommend having any water
intrusion evaluated and areas treated by a waterproofing contractor as needed.
(2) Several minor cracks were noted in the foundation walls on home. Monitor any crack in concrete for further
movement, settlement or water intrusion which would require further evaluation and repair.
(2) There was a minor repair to a rafter at the front of the roof. The repair appeared adequate. Recommend having
(1) Paint cans were found in the attic. Paint is flammable. Recommend having removed from the interior of the
(2) There was a considerable amount of debris, abandoned items and materials in the attic area. Recommend
having the attic cleared of all personal and abandoned items.
(2) Floor joists had a foreign, mold-like substance growing on them. Recommendations within this report concerning
the crawl space, water intrusion and water management should be closely followed.
Strong recommendation that this substance be further evaluated by a Mold Inspection company and be immediately
re mediated and clearance testing performed based on the recommendations of the Mold Inspection company and
(3) There were gaps along the edges of the crawlspace walls. these could be sources of infiltration for insects and
should be sealed accordingly. Recommend having a pest control contractor add exclusionary devices to seal
PLUMBING DRAIN, WASTE AND VENT SYSTEMS
(2) The drain lines in the home were noted as a mixture of cast iron and PVC. Cast iron was very common during
the time this home was built, but is often susceptible to tree root infiltration and damage. We cannot verify the
(1) The toilet in the primary bath was loose at the floor connection. This can result in sewer gasses entering room.
Recommend consulting plumber to remove toilet, replace wax ring and re-secure to floor flange properly
(2) The toilet in the primary bath was not sealed to the floor as is required by the plumbing code. Recommend
having a contractor properly seal all toilets to the floor with a silicone material to prevent the build up of debris and
(2) The caulking on the window around the hallway bathtub area is coming loose. This entire window frame is
damaged as viewed from the outside. Recommend repairing or replacing as needed and then seal and paint as
(3) All intersections in tile and tub should be routinely monitored and caulked when necessary. Tubs should be
completely filled with water prior to caulking to separate tub from tile to allow caulking to completely fill space
(4) The tub was noted as having a couple of chips in the finish. These will deteriorate over time if left unattended.
Recommend having a contractor properly repair the tubs as noted to leakage.
(6) The bathtub tile was noted as having missing grout joints in various locations and was in need of being re-
grouted. Recommend having a contractor grout as needed.
(2) The bathtub tile was noted as having missing grout joints in various locations and was in need of being re-
grouted. Recommend having a contractor grout as needed.
HOT WATER SYSTEMS, CONTROLS, CHIMNEYS, FLUES AND VENTS
(2) While electrical water heaters are very efficient, it is possible to install a 220 Volt timer on the electric water
heater to control the unnecessary heating of water when the homeowner's family was away and increase the energy
This is often very effective for families that are away during the day. The timer will shut down the heating elements
during common work day hours and sleeping hours as programmed by the homeowner.
(3) There as excessive and damaged wire at the water heater. Recommend having an electrician properly wire and
(4) There is an abandoned water heater in the crawlspace. Recommend having a contractor remove.
The overhead electrical conductors were noted as extending through the trees and bushes in the front of the home.
Recommend having branches trimmed to limit the potential damage to the electrical conductors.
CONNECTED DEVICES AND FIXTURES (Observed from a representative number operation of ceiling fans,
lighting fixtures, switches and receptacles located inside the building, and on the building's exterior walls)
(1) There are 2 fans in the crawlspace, and only one was running. This is likely to remove moisture from the
crawlspace. Recommend having an electrician repair or replace fan that is not working.
(2) Several lights in the home were either not operating or needed repair at the time of the inspection including but
not limited to; the light in the bedrooms, and bathroom. Recommend having a licensed electrician evaluate and
repair or replace all damaged or defective devices.
(3) There is a switch in the attic labeled "Back Yard Flood Lights". Recommend having an electrician further
(4) An open junction box was noted in the crawlspace area above the abandoned water heater. Recommend having
an electrician properly install junction box covers where needed for safety.
(1) There is an old abandoned alarm panel in the primary bedroom. Recommend having removed if no longer in
(2) The alarm panel at the front door has a trouble code regarding a battery that is low. Recommend having alarm
system installation contractor evaluate alarm system and repair as needed.
The door bell button at the front door was noted as broken and in need of being repaired.
The furnace was older it was manufactured in 2019 and did not appear to have been professionally serviced
Recommend having the HVAC system professionally cleaned, serviced and evaluated by an HVAC company
including but not limited to: Check all ductwork and connections, lubricating moving parts, tighten all electrical
connections, check all controls, fans and evaluation of the heating elements, refrigerant levels, clean condenser
coils, check clean cooling coil and linesets.
(1) The electrical box for the AC service shut off is loose from the wall. Recommend having an electrician secure.
(2) The compressors outside (AC unit) is older (2019) and may last a few years more but may not. This unit appears
to be currently cooling the home as needed. It cannot be determined how long the AC will last before a replacement
is necessary. Preventive maintenance and annual servicing can prolong the longevity of the unit.
Recommend having an HVAC contractor evaluate the entire system and repair or replace components as needed.
Various areas of the attic insulation is compressed and was in need of being touched up or added to.
It is recommended to have at least a minimum of R-30 installed in attic spaces. some of the insulation appeared to
have had items stored on top and was compressed which diminishes the effectiveness.
Recommend having insulation contractor provide the insulation recommended which would be approximately 10
inches deep providing an R value of 30.
The floor system is not insulated. Heat loss can occur more on this home than one that is properly insulated.
Recommend having a duct cleaning company or a general contractor clean all lint from the dryer exhaust duct
(2) The sides of the dishwasher were noted as not aligned with the cabinets which resulted in a gap and appeared
incomplete. Recommend having a contractor properly install the dishwasher.
The disposal was noted as very loud and seemed like there was some metal inside. Recommend having a plumber
repair or replace the disposal as noted.
(1) The intersection between the kitchen countertops and the back splash was cracked and in need of caulking. All
intersections should be routinely caulked to prevent water intrusion into the cabinets.
(2) Some of the doors on the kitchen cabinets were misaligned and not properly covering the opening. Recommend
having a cabinet installer properly repair all cabinet doors and drawers for optimal appearance as well as function.
The rim of the granite at the kitchen sink currently overhangs the rolled edge of the sink thus causing a concealed
fouling area which could be potentially a health hazard. Currently the edge was in need of being cleaned.
Recommend having a contractor thoroughly clean the connection point and properly seal overhanging edge of
granite with a silicone material to prevent the build up of debris and germs.
Inspectors are not required to report on the following: Life expectancy of any component or system; The causes of the need
for a repair; The methods, materials, and costs of corrections; The suitability of the property for any specialized use;
Compliance or non-compliance with codes, ordinances, statutes, regulatory requirements or restrictions; The market value of
the property or its marketability; The advisability or inadvisability of purchase of the property; Any component or system that
was not observed; The presence or absence of pests such as wood damaging organisms, rodents, or insects; or Cosmetic
items, underground items, or items not permanently installed. Inspectors are not required to: Offer warranties or guarantees
of any kind; Calculate the strength, adequacy, or efficiency of any system or component; Enter any area or perform any
procedure that may damage the property or its components or be dangerous to the inspector or other persons; Operate any
system or component that is shut down or otherwise inoperable; Operate any system or component that does not respond to
normal operating controls; Disturb insulation, move personal items, panels, furniture, equipment, plant life, soil, snow, ice, or
debris that obstructs access or visibility; Determine the presence or absence of any suspected adverse environmental
condition or hazardous substance, including but not limited to mold, toxins, carcinogens, noise, contaminants in the building
or in soil, water, and air; Determine the effectiveness of any system installed to control or remove suspected hazardous
substances; Predict future condition, including but not limited to failure of components; Since this report is provided for the
specific benefit of the customer(s), secondary readers of this information should hire a licensed inspector to perform an
inspection to meet their specific needs and to obtain current information concerning this property.
